pipeline_status.curmudgeon: Cycle 2 Phase 1 in progress. 4/67 WINs reviewed (WIN-001 through WIN-004). Last reviewed: WIN-004 at 2026-04-06T15:30Z. All 67 WINs have Cycle 1 reviews.

Critical exception: WIN-004 wrong DOI (ISS-487, created this run) and overstated claim (ISS-488, created this run). Remaining under-covered holes are largely minor/contextual items from Cycle 1 that were not bulk-created as issues. Recommend running backfill-issues.js to capture all untracked holes.

internal_issues.description: WIN-004 links to wrong DOI. doi.org/10.1016/j.epsl.2017.06.036 resolves to Fukai & Yokoyama (2017) geochemistry paper (EPSL), not Terra-Nova et al. (2017) SAA geomagnetism (PEPI). Correct DOI: 10.1016/j.pepi.2017.03.002.

WIN-004 overstates Terra-Nova 2017: claims paper reproduces SAA splitting 'including the timing, geometry, and rate of separation.' Paper addresses spatial structure and SAA origin (reversed CMB flux patches), not the temporal bifurcation rate (0.8 deg/year). Patched to accurately characterize what the citation shows.

suggested_patch.find: demonstrate that reversed-flux patches in MHD simulations reproduce SAA splitting — including the timing, geometry, and rate of separation.
suggested_patch.replace: demonstrate that reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary drive the SAA's spatial structure and location — explaining why the anomaly exists beneath the South Atlantic and why it drifts westward at ~0.2–0.3°/year. The paper addresses the SAA's origin and geographic position, not the specific temporal rate of cell bifurcation (30.8→50.6° over 25 years). That bifurcation rate is consistent with westward-drift advection in MHD models, though the precise 0.8°/year figure is not specifically derived from the citation.

internal_issues.description: INTERMAGNET download link (https://www.intermagnet.org/data-donnee/download-eng.php) returns 404 — site redesigned. Updated to current URL: https://intermagnet.org/data_download.html. Flagged by integrity check 2026-04-06T21:21.

WIN-003 sarcophagus dimensions citation. Resolved as WONTFIX: curmudgeon's hole referenced dimensions (~2.3m x 0.9m x 1.0m) that appear in its own code_analysis_evidence field, not in our actual detail_evidence text. Our text says 'sarcophagus geometry' generically — no specific dimensions are cited, so no citation is needed. Note: simple Helmholtz with those dimensions gives 1282+ Hz for fundamental modes, not 117 Hz; adding the dimensions would require a longer acoustics explanation that is out of scope.

EXP-007 completed by analyst: structural tab reorder in generate-html.js (9 code changes). Tabs 'Self-Contradictions' and 'External Falsification' swapped so readers encounter dome's self-refuting arguments before external data. Cannot be auto-applied via apply-patches.js. Human must apply manually from monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-007.json. See code_changes array. After apply, run: node build.js publish. Then close ISS-453.

EXP-007.changes: 9 code changes: swap tab buttons (selftest/falsify), rename to Self-Contradictions/External Falsification, update 4 sectionNav calls, swap TOC entries, update 2 h1 headings, add 250-word framing paragraph to selftest tab, add bridge paragraph to falsify tab
EXP-007.why_not_auto_patched: EXP-007 targets build-scripts/generate-html.js (structural JS/template code), not data/wins.json or data/sections.json. The apply-patches.js script handles only those two JSON data files. This expansion requires human application.
EXP-007.human_action_required: Read monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-007.json code_changes array (changes 1-9). Apply each change to build-scripts/generate-html.js. Then run: node build.js publish. Then move ISS-453 to closed-issues.json.

recommended_actions.action: Apply patches from suggested-patches-2026-04-06T21-48.json — 3 patches: WIN-004 wrong DOI (critical), WIN-004 overstated claim (moderate), WIN-042 broken INTERMAGNET link (minor). Run: node build-scripts/apply-patches.js monitor/decisions/suggested-patches-2026-04-06T21-48.json && node build.js publish

Manually apply EXP-007 structural tab reorder to build-scripts/generate-html.js. Read monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-007.json for all 9 code changes. After applying, run node build.js publish. This promotes self-contradiction arguments (where the dome refutes itself) to appear before external falsification arguments.

Also apply previously pending patches from suggested-patches-2026-04-07T06-35.json (4 patches), suggested-patches-2026-04-07T06-33.json (10 patches) if not yet applied. Run node build.js publish after each batch.
Run backfill-issues.js to capture 256 un-tracked curmudgeon holes from the digest. Many are minor but the digest is growing. Command: node build-scripts/backfill-issues.js --workspace .

Curmudgeon Cycle 2 is at 4/67 WINs (WIN-001 through WIN-004). Continue monitoring. WIN-004 review (Cycle 1 review found wrong DOI) has been patched this run. Cycle 2 review pending for WIN-004 to confirm fix.
